mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer price index indicate strong demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index signals expanding manufacturing capacity and economic momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle expansions, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate income, which supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months with payroll growth, or the correlation coefficient between payroll changes and 20-day returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1200 thousand units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 60% of occurrences, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising federal funds rates and accelerating inflation indicates restrictive monetary policy that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive forward returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms robust economic expansion, which sustains corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during stable growth regimes.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during mid-cycle expansions, or the mean forward return during high-GDP quarters is not significantly different from zero with t-test p-value above 0.05.
